mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0% signals labor market slack that suppresses consumer spending growth and corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real short-term interest rates combined with declining real savings indicate tightening financial conditions that reduce household liquidity and equity demand, producing negative forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when RSAFS increases and FEDFUNDS rises simultaneously (out-of-sample accuracy < 45%).

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month contractions in industrial production reflect immediate supply-side shocks that trigger risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and supports corporate revenue forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 20-day SPX direction (Rank IC consistently near zero across rolling 2-year windows).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts dropping below 1.8 million units signals a collapse in construction demand and related supply chains, forecasting negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 70% of quarters following HOUST readings below 1800.0.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation alongside decelerating real GDP growth creates stagflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, driving negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The CPIAUCSL-GDPC1 divergence fails to predict negative returns when inflation rises and GDP growth slows (accuracy < 40% over 3-year samples).

mechanism_narrative: Rapid month-over-month increases in real savings indicate precautionary hoarding during economic uncertainty, reducing immediate consumption and triggering short-term negative equity returns.
failure_condition: RSAFS surges are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in > 65% of observations during uncertainty periods.
